channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Telemedicine in Venezuela: A Technical Guide for Clinics in 2026
item.description: In Venezuela, telemedicine is no longer a futuristic promise. It's an operational tool for handling clinical follow-ups, expanding coverage, and reducing friction in a system where distance, time,...

item.title: SSO, SAML, OIDC, and SCIM: What Actually Happens When You Click "Sign in with Google"
item.description: You click "Sign in with Google" on GitHub. Seconds later, you are in. No new account. No new password. That felt simple. The system behind it is not. This article breaks down the four protocols...

item.title: Mastering Next.js 16 Server Actions & Forms: The Future of Full-Stack React | Muhammad Arslan
item.description: The landscape of web development is shifting. In 2026, the boundary between the client and the server has all but vanished. Next.js 16 Server Actions represent the pinnacle of this evolution,...

item.title: Enterprise Laravel API Development: Best Practices for Performance, Security, and Scale | Muhammad Arslan
item.description: Enterprise application development in 2026 demands APIs that are not just functional, but resilient, blazingly fast, and capable of scaling seamlessly under heavy loads. While Laravel is often...

item.title: How I Turned an Image Into a 3D Model in Minutes With AI
item.description: Creating a 3D model usually starts with a lot of friction. You need a concept. Then a reference. Then modeling. Then cleanup. And if you are doing all of that in a traditional workflow, even a...

item.title: Why Pure Rust WASM Is Harder Than It Looks
item.description: Most Rust developers expect the pitch to hold: compile to WebAssembly, ship to the browser, no C required. Add the wasm32-unknown-unknown target, run wasm-pack build, and you're done. Then you try...

item.title: Platform Stores Are a Dead End for Crypto Payments
item.description: The Problem We Were Actually Solving Our company, a platform for creators, needed a payment system that would work seamlessly worldwide, without relying on legacy banking systems. We wanted to...

item.title: The VLA Testing Pipeline in Mano-AFK: When AI Agents QA Their Own Work
item.description: AI coding tools have gotten remarkably good at generating code. You describe what you want, and within minutes you have functions, components, even entire applications scaffolded out. But there's...

item.title: LeetCode Solution: 10. Regular Expression Matching
item.description: Mastering Regular Expressions: A Deep Dive into LeetCode 10 with Dynamic Programming Hey there, fellow coders! 👋 Are you ready to tackle one of LeetCode's classic challenges that might seem...

item.title: IPv4 Geolocation and Leasing: A Practical Guide for Network Operators
item.description: Geolocation questions come up regularly when operators start leasing IPv4 blocks. Does the subnet show up in the right country? How long until databases reflect the correct location? What's the...

item.title: When Identity is the Attack Path
item.description: Consider a cached access key on a single Windows machine. It got there the way most cached credentials do - a user logged in, and the key stored itself automatically. Standard AWS behavior. No one...

item.title: Climate tech companies are pivoting to critical minerals
item.description: We’re over a year into the second Trump administration here in the US, and support for climate causes is weak. But climate tech companies are finding ways to survive and even thrive in this new...

item.title: ‘Fuck you, Bambu’: How one private message could change the face of 3D printing
item.description: Bambu Lab makes the best, most accessible 3D printers yet, but that reputation is suddenly under siege. It all started when Paweł Jarczak received a private message from the company on Reddit...

item.title: Meta lays off thousands of employees to offset AI investments
item.description: Meta has reportedly notified thousands of employees that they've been laid off as the company attempts to compensate for its hefty AI investments. In an email from Meta management shared by...

item.title: Tech researchers are suing the Trump administration over the future of online safety
item.description: Since its earliest days back in office, the Trump administration has been going after researchers who study and try to counter hate speech, harassment, propaganda, and disinformation online. Now,...

item.title: 9-Year-Old Linux Kernel Flaw Enables Root Command Execution on Major Distros
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a vulnerability in the Linux kernel that remained undetected for nine years. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-46333 (CVSS score: 5.5), is...

item.title: GitHub Internal Repositories Breached via Malicious Nx Console VS Code Extension
item.description: GitHub on Wednesday officially confirmed that the breach of its internal repositories was the result of a compromise of an employee device involving a poisoned version of the Nx Console Microsoft...

item.title: Highly Critical Drupal Core Flaw Exposes PostgreSQL Sites to RCE Attacks
item.description: Drupal has released security updates for a "highly critical" security vulnerability in Drupal Core that could be exploited by attackers to achieve remote code execution, privilege escalation, or...

item.title: In SpaceX’s IPO, Elon Musk is a risk factor
item.description: The SpaceX IPO is here, and it's more than just an historic public offering that could make Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire. It also reveals more ways in which Elon Musk's companies...

item.title: SpaceX Listed Grok’s ‘Spicy’ Mode as a Risk in Its IPO Filing
item.description: The rocket company has set aside more than $500 million for potential litigation losses, in part to account for complaints alleging that Grok created sexualized images.

item.title: Jensen Huang says he’s found a ‘brand new’ $200B market for Nvidia
item.description: The next big thing for Nvidia will be CPUs for AI agents, $200 billion worth, CEO Jensen Huang predicts.

item.title: Anthropic says it’s about to have its first profitable quarter
item.description: Anthropic has told its investors that it will more than double revenue to around $10.9 billion in its second quarter.

item.title: SpaceX Is Spending $2.8 Billion to Buy Gas Turbines for Its AI Data Centers
item.description: The investment comes as Elon Musk’s AI unit faces complaints about the carbon-emitting units and looks to become a big player in cloud computing.

item.title: Clouted wants to take the guesswork out of making short videos go viral
item.description: The video clipping startup raised a $7 million seed round led by Slow Ventures.

item.title: xAI burned $6.4B last year — SpaceX’s IPO filing shows why the spending is far from over
item.description: SpaceX's IPO filing reveals xAI lost $6.4 billion in 2025 while planning a massive Grok expansion — offering the first public look at Elon Musk's AI financials and more details about his ambitions.

item.title: Nvidia posts another record quarter, reveals $43B of holdings in startups
item.description: Nvidia announced another record revenue figure after market close on Wednesday, but forecasted that revenue growth would slow in the following quarter.

item.title: Musk’s xAI is being sued over its data center generators — now it’s buying $2.8B more
item.description: Elon Musk's xAI said it will buy $2.8 billion worth of natural gas turbines over the next three years, according to SpaceX's IPO filing.

item.title: SpaceX just filed for what could be the biggest IPO ever
item.description: Elon Musk's final frontier is officially open for business now that SpaceX has formally filed its S-1 prospectus with the SEC. That kicks off what could be the largest initial public offering ever...

item.title: ‘Solve all diseases,’ you say?
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life. This...

item.title: Here are 42 of our favorite deals from REI’s massive Anniversary Sale
item.description: REI’s annual Anniversary Sale — the retailer’s biggest of the year — has arrived, bringing with it discounts on all kinds of outdoor essentials. If you’ve got a camping trip coming up, now is a...
